description_long: This is a stately, rectangular manor house flanked by two smaller outbuildings, all set around a central open courtyard. The main house is three stories tall, painted white, and features a steep, gray gabled roof with multiple chimneys and dormer windows. The facade is symmetrical, with evenly spaced windows and a prominent central entryway. The outbuildings have red-tiled gable roofs and white walls, echoing the main house's style but on a smaller scale. The grounds include a paved courtyard, grassy areas, and some landscaping with trees and shrubs. The overall setting suggests a historic estate or manor, likely from the Colonial or early Georgian period.
description: A large, white three-story manor house with a steep gray gabled roof, flanked by two smaller red-roofed outbuildings around a central courtyard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main house's symmetrical facade, central entry, and both outbuildings framing the courtyard.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side of the main house and the depth of the courtyard, with both outbuildings visible.
description_view_2: Rear view of the main house, showing the back facade and the outbuildings from behind.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the gabled end of the main house and the alignment of the outbuildings along the courtyard.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with steps, simple door, and minimal decorative elements
